title: Mildly Spicy Ethiopian Lentils Tibs
description: A comforting Ethiopian dish featuring chilli bean paste and high protein lentils, served in a yeasty and glassy jelly form, this tibs recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat and saute the onion until it is translucent.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the chilli bean paste and berbere spice and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lentils, water, salt, and pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Use a spoon to deglaze the fond from the bottom of the pot and stir it back into the lentils.
In a small bowl, mix the yeast with 1 tablespoon of warm water and let it sit for 5 minutes, until it is activated and frothy.
Stir the yeast mixture into the lentils and continue to simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened and formed a glassy jelly.
Serve the tibs hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
